title: Factorial dominates 2^n
informal_statement: For every natural number n, 2^n <= (n+1)!. Equivalent, via the substitution n -> n - 1, to the more commonly stated "n! >= 2^(n-1) for n >= 1"; phrased with a shift by one to avoid Nat truncated subtraction entirely.
informal_proof_idea: Induction on n. Base case n = 0: 2^0 = 1 <= 1! = 1 by norm_num. Successor case: given ih : 2^n <= (n+1)!, rewrite (n+1+1)! via Nat.factorial_succ to (n+1+1) * (n+1)! and 2^(n+1) via pow_succ to 2^n * 2, then nlinarith closes 2^n * 2 <= (n+1+1) * (n+1)! from ih together with Nat.factorial_pos (n+1) (needed so nlinarith can certify the (n+1)! >= 1 slack that makes the (n+2) multiplier dominate the factor of 2).
formal_statement_pp: theorem factorial_ge_two_pow : ∀ n : ℕ, 2 ^ n ≤ Nat.factorial (n + 1)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 538ea8b6), kernel_verified on the first Solve attempt. Cross-references packets/elementary/number_theory/factorial_pos.v1.json and factorial_le.v1.json (see CROSS_DOMAIN.md). Builds on the induction curriculum lane, the smallest domain in the corpus at the time of authoring.
